mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show positive direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, equity markets price in imminent monetary easing, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 40% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real GDP and retail sales signal a synchronized demand contraction that forces earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 52%, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply-side bottlenecks, a rebound in the industrial production index indicates capacity utilization recovery, which lifts corporate margins and produces positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day returns in >60% of post-shock recovery periods, or the mechanism shows negative predictive power during inflationary supply constraints.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index surpasses 290, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, triggering immediate risk-off flows that depress S&P 500 prices over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in >50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 290, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates a robust labor market that supports consumer spending, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline predicts negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over single predictors.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts reflect weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, which drags on economic growth expectations and produces negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative HOUST changes correlate with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in >60% of observations, or the predictive IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling test windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 100, it signals a contraction in manufacturing output that triggers recession fears, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in >50% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of stable monetary policy.
